description: NCII sector regulatory overlap matrix — shows which regulatory frameworks apply to each NCII sector in addition to Act 854

regulatoryOverlaps.notes: Government agencies must comply with DGSM requirements in addition to Act 854 for ICT security

regulatoryOverlaps.fullName: Rangka Kerja Keselamatan Siber Sektor Awam
regulatoryOverlaps.notes: Public Sector Cybersecurity Framework — pre-existing framework now supplemented by Act 854
Government agencies were previously exempt from PDPA but 2024 amendments may expand applicability
BNM-regulated financial institutions must comply with both RMiT and Act 854; RMiT requirements generally align with or exceed Act 854 obligations
Financial data processing triggers PDPA obligations alongside NCII duties; dual breach notification requirements apply

regulatoryOverlaps.fullName: Securities Commission Malaysia Guidelines on Technology Risk Management
regulatoryOverlaps.notes: Capital market intermediaries face additional SC technology risk requirements

regulatoryOverlaps.fullName: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
regulatoryOverlaps.notes: Card processing entities must also maintain PCI DSS compliance
Aviation NCII entities must meet ICAO cybersecurity requirements for air navigation and airport systems

regulatoryOverlaps.fullName: IMO Guidelines on Maritime Cyber Risk Management
regulatoryOverlaps.notes: Maritime NCII entities must address IMO cyber risk management guidelines for port and vessel systems
Aviation security programme includes cybersecurity elements for critical aviation systems
Defence NCII information may be classified under OSA, creating overlapping secrecy obligations with s49
NSC directives on critical infrastructure protection supplement Act 854 requirements
Military NCII entities must also comply with defence-specific information security management standards
Telecommunications providers face CMA licence conditions alongside Act 854 NCII obligations
ICT infrastructure standards set by MCMC supplement Act 854 security standards
Telcos processing subscriber personal data face dual obligations
Enhanced penalties under Part IX amendments apply to offences against ICT NCII
Healthcare NCII systems processing patient data face dual Act 854 and PDPA obligations; health data is sensitive personal data under PDPA

regulatoryOverlaps.fullName: Malaysian Medical Association Data Protection Guidelines
regulatoryOverlaps.notes: Professional medical data handling standards supplement regulatory requirements

regulatoryOverlaps.fullName: Private Healthcare Facilities and Services Act 1998
regulatoryOverlaps.notes: Private healthcare facilities face additional licensing conditions for IT systems
Water operators face SPAN licensing conditions alongside Act 854 NCII obligations
Water treatment SCADA/ICS systems require specialized OT security standards beyond IT-focused requirements
Cyber incidents affecting water treatment could have environmental compliance implications
Energy utilities face Energy Commission licensing conditions alongside Act 854 obligations
Gas infrastructure operators face parallel regulatory requirements
OT/SCADA systems in energy sector should align with IEC 62443 alongside Act 854 requirements
International reference standard for power grid cybersecurity; informs local CoP development

agriculture-and-plantation.sectorLead: Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security

regulatoryOverlaps.notes: Food safety systems disruption could trigger food safety obligations
Commodity trading systems may face MPOB regulatory requirements alongside NCII obligations
Agricultural biosecurity monitoring systems are potential NCII assets
Nuclear research facilities face additional safety and security requirements for control systems

regulatoryOverlaps.fullName: National Science and Research Council Act
regulatoryOverlaps.notes: Research infrastructure cybersecurity intersects with research governance requirements
Research institutions processing personal data face dual obligations for data management systems
Education systems processing student and staff personal data face dual Act 854 and PDPA obligations
National education examination and management systems are potential NCII assets
University IT governance requirements supplement NCII obligations for higher education institutions

education.sectorWarning: Education is NOT listed as an NCII sector in the Schedule to Act 854. This entry is retained for reference only.

trade-industry-and-economy.sectorLead: Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry (MITI)

regulatoryOverlaps.notes: Trade facilitation systems (MyTrade, customs portals) face customs regulatory requirements alongside NCII obligations
Investment processing systems are potential NCII assets under trade and economic infrastructure
Trade systems processing business and personal data face dual Act 854 and PDPA obligations
